code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or product failure
    - Safety hazards/defects requiring return
    - Phrases: "terrible," "dangerous," "avoid"
    - Negative updates overriding initial positives

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Functional flaws outweigh positives
    - Core purpose failure despite partial utility
    - Language: "frustrating," "poor quality," "misleading"

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons without dominance
    - Works adequately with notable compromises
    - Phrases: "okay IF...", "better than nothing"

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Clear satisfaction with specific critiques
    - Minor usability/durability issues
    - "Deducting one star for X but still good"
    - Requires user effort to achieve full function

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic endorsement despite minor flaws
    - Explicit "5 stars overall" declarations
    - Exceeds specific needs/use cases
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "perfect solution"
    - Strong recommendation overrides isolated critiques

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - "Still 5 stars despite..." → 5
    - Multi-feature excellence → 5 (1 weak aspect)
    - Requires cleaning/breaking-in → 4 if satisfactory post-effort
    - Over-specced but functional → 5 if user satisfied
    - Disposable/single-use praise → 5 if meets need perfectly
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: UPDATE: Lasted only 2 months before breaking. Not worth repairing.
<Reasoning>Negative update shows product failure. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: Blades jam constantly despite good chopping. Too frustrating for daily use.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good performance.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Does the job but feels flimsy. Okay for light use but not heavy-duty tasks.
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with quality concerns. Mixed sentiment balances to neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: These containers are perfect for leftovers! I don't mind losing them - cheap and effective.
<Reasoning>High praise for specific use case despite disposable nature. Perfect solution warrants 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Taller than needed but seals perfectly. Deducting one star for size mismatch.
<Reasoning>Minor sizing issue doesn't override functional excellence. Clear star deduction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - guest bed perfection!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect score. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Towels require 2 washes to absorb well. Deducting one star but still great post-break-in.
<Reasoning>Initial deficiency mitigated through effort. Satisfied with minor reservation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Tricky assembly but worth it! Best purchase this year.
<Reasoning>Strong recommendation overrides initial difficulty. Transformative praise.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Sparks flew on first use. Immediate fire hazard!
<Reasoning>Safety failure overrides all other considerations.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (34 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 12
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I was actually looking for smaller pie cover; really only wanting something that would cover and keep my pumpkin pie from drying out when it was in the fridge.

For that purpose this Pie holder is overkill. It's LARGE. It's tall enough for meringues ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [4, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 34 errors, 13 borderline out of 80 examples."}
